supplementEntry.name: DIM (3,3'-Diindolylmethane)
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Multiple small-to-moderate RCTs (n=12-551) demonstrate estrogen metabolism modulation (2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio shift). Phase I/II cancer prevention trials show biomarker changes but no direct cancer outcome data. One systematic review of 22 human trials confirms safety but notes absence of efficacy evidence for treating established cancers. Cervical dysplasia trials show mixed results.

nonCognitive: Anti-inflammatory effects via NF-kB and AhR modulation
Antioxidant defense via Nrf2 pathway activation

supplementEntry.dosageRange: 100-300mg daily (absorption-enhanced formulation); typical clinical dose 150mg BID
supplementEntry.optimalDuration: 4-12 weeks for estrogen metabolite shifts; 3-12 months in cancer prevention trials
studyPopulations: Women taking tamoxifen for breast cancer
mechanismsOfAction: Modulates estrogen metabolism by shifting 2-hydroxylation:16a-hydroxylation ratio via CYP1A1/CYP1A2 induction
Aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AhR) agonist/modulator affecting xenobiotic metabolism
NF-kB signaling inhibition reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine expression (TNF-a, IL-6, IL-1b)
Nrf2/ARE pathway activation upregulating detoxification enzymes (NQO1, GST, HO-1)
Androgen receptor downregulation and anti-androgenic activity
BRCA1 gene expression upregulation in estrogen-responsive tissues
Inhibition of Akt/mTOR and MAPK proliferative signaling pathways

commonSideEffects: Generally well-tolerated at doses up to 300mg/day
Mild GI upset (nausea) at higher doses
contraindications: Hormone-sensitive cancers (use only under medical supervision)
Concurrent use with tamoxifen (reduces tamoxifen metabolite levels)
drugInteractions: Reduces plasma tamoxifen, endoxifen, and 4-OH tamoxifen levels (Thomson 2017 RCT)
May alter estradiol exposure in women on transdermal estradiol patches
Potential CYP1A2 and CYP3A4 inducer affecting drug metabolism

effectSizes.estrogenMetaboliteRatioShift: 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio increase of +3.2 vs -0.7 placebo (P<0.001, n=98; Thomson 2017)
effectSizes.shbgIncrease: Significant SHBG increase vs placebo in tamoxifen-treated women (P<0.05, n=98; Thomson 2017)
effectSizes.cervicalDysplasiaCINRegression: Complete CIN regression significantly higher vs placebo at 100mg and 200mg vaginal DIM (P<0.05, n=multisite; Ashrafian 2015)
effectSizes.tamoxifenMetaboliteReduction: Appreciable reduction in endoxifen and 4-OH tamoxifen levels with DIM co-administration (P<0.05, n=98; Thomson 2017)
effectSizes.bodyFatReduction: Greater body fat percentage reduction vs placebo (non-significant trend, n=60; Godinez-Martinez 2023)
effectSizes.psaResponse: 1/12 patients achieved 50% PSA decline at 225mg BID (Phase I; Heath 2010)
effectSizes.thyroidEstrogenModulation: Favorable 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 shift in thyroid tissue, serum, and urine (n=7; Rajoria 2011)

keyCitations.title: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial of diindolylmethane for breast cancer biomarker modulation in patients taking tamoxifen

3,3'-Diindolylmethane modulates estrogen metabolism in patients with thyroid proliferative disease: a pilot study

Chemopreventive properties of 3,3'-diindolylmethane in breast cancer: evidence from experimental and human studies
Attenuation of multi-targeted proliferation-linked signaling by 3,3'-diindolylmethane (DIM): from bench to clinic

mechanisms.mechanism: Modulates estrogen metabolism by shifting 2-hydroxylation:16a-hydroxylation ratio via CYP1A1/CYP1A2 induction

evidence.title: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial of diindolylmethane for breast cancer biomarker modulation in patients taking tamoxifen
evidence.findings: DIM 150mg BID significantly increased urinary 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio (+3.2 vs -0.7 placebo, P<0.001). SHBG also increased vs placebo. Demonstrates robust CYP1A1-mediated estrogen metabolism shift in humans.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT in tamoxifen-treated breast cancer patients

evidence.title: 3,3'-Diindolylmethane modulates estrogen metabolism in patients with thyroid proliferative disease: a pilot study
evidence.findings: DIM 300mg/day was detectable in thyroid tissue, serum, and urine. Favorable shift in 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio consistent with antiestrogenic activity. Demonstrates tissue-level penetration and estrogen metabolism modulation.
evidence.methodology: Open-label pilot study in female patients with thyroid proliferative disease

mechanisms.mechanism: Aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AhR) agonist/modulator affecting xenobiotic metabolism
mechanisms.tissueTarget: Multiple tissues including liver and immune cells
mechanisms.target: Multiple tissues including liver and immune cells

evidence.title: Attenuation of multi-targeted proliferation-linked signaling by 3,3'-diindolylmethane (DIM): from bench to clinic
evidence.findings: DIM acts as an AhR modulator, abrogating TCDD-induced recruitment of AhR to the COX-2 promoter. Represses COX-2 mRNA and protein expression. Naturally occurring AhR modulators like DIM overcome epigenetic activation of inflammatory pathways by environmental AhR agonists.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of preclinical and clinical evidence on DIM signaling pathways

evidence.title: Unveiling the multifaceted pharmacological actions of indole-3-carbinol and diindolylmethane: a comprehensive review
evidence.findings: DIM and AhR have strong binding affinity. DIM reduces oxidative stress through Nrf2/ARE/HO-1 pathway activation and mitigates inflammation by blocking TNF-a, ILs, TLR, RANKL, JAK/STAT, and NF-kB translocation. Disrupts AhR/NF-kB P65 interaction.
evidence.methodology: Comprehensive pharmacological review of I3C and DIM mechanisms

mechanisms.mechanism: NF-kB signaling inhibition reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine expression

evidence.title: Attenuation of multi-targeted proliferation-linked signaling by 3,3'-diindolylmethane (DIM): from bench to clinic
evidence.findings: DIM inhibits NF-kB translocation and activation through multiple upstream mechanisms. Blocks TNF-a, interleukins, TLR, and RANKL signaling. Disrupts AhR/NF-kB P65 crosstalk to attenuate inflammatory amplification.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of DIM molecular targets in inflammation and cancer

mechanisms.mechanism: Nrf2/ARE pathway activation upregulating detoxification enzymes

evidence.title: Unveiling the multifaceted pharmacological actions of indole-3-carbinol and diindolylmethane: a comprehensive review
evidence.findings: DIM stimulates Nrf2-regulated promoters including NQO1 (oxidoreductase), GSTa1 (glutathione S-transferase), and x-CT (cystine/glutamate transporter). Nrf2 release and nuclear translocation activates transcription of detoxification and antioxidant proteins. Upstream BRCA-1 and protein kinases involved.
evidence.methodology: Comprehensive pharmacological review

mechanisms.mechanism: Androgen receptor downregulation and anti-androgenic activity

evidence.title: A phase I dose-escalation study of oral BR-DIM (BioResponse 3,3'-Diindolylmethane) in castrate-resistant, non-metastatic prostate cancer
evidence.findings: BR-DIM modulates estrogen metabolism and acts as an anti-androgen downregulating the androgen receptor and PSA. One patient achieved 50% PSA decline at 225mg. MTD established at 300mg. Recommended phase II dose: 225mg BID.
evidence.methodology: Phase I dose-escalation in castrate-resistant non-metastatic prostate cancer patients

evidence.title: Phase Ib placebo-controlled, tissue biomarker trial of diindolylmethane (BR-DIMNG) in patients with prostate cancer who are undergoing prostatectomy
evidence.findings: DIM detected in only 7/28 prostate tissue specimens. Urinary estrone ratio showed meaningful differences between placebo and high-dose groups. Confirmed feasibility of preoperative supplementation window but suggested poor tissue penetration with this formulation.
evidence.methodology: Randomized, placebo-controlled Phase Ib trial in prostatectomy patients

benefits.specificClaim: Shifts estrogen metabolism toward favorable 2-hydroxylation pathway
benefits.replicationStatus: Replicated across multiple RCTs and populations

evidence.title: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial of diindolylmethane for breast cancer biomarker modulation in patients taking tamoxifen
evidence.findings: DIM 150mg BID produced significant 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio increase (+3.2 vs -0.7 placebo, P<0.001). SHBG significantly increased. No change in breast density by mammography or MRI.
evidence.methodology: 12-month double-blind RCT in tamoxifen-treated breast cancer patients

evidence.title: 3,3'-Diindolylmethane modulates estrogen metabolism in patients with thyroid proliferative disease: a pilot study
evidence.findings: DIM 300mg/day increased 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio in thyroid tissue, serum, and urine. DIM was sequestered in thyroid tissue confirming tissue-level activity.
evidence.methodology: Open-label pilot in patients with thyroid proliferative disease

evidence.title: Effectiveness of 3,3'-Diindolylmethane supplements on favoring the benign estrogen metabolism pathway and decreasing body fat in premenopausal women
evidence.findings: DIM 75mg/day (300mg DIM-BR) did not significantly increase estrogen metabolite ratio at 30 days but showed non-significant positive trend 30 days post-supplementation. Greater body fat reduction in DIM group vs placebo.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in premenopausal women with low estrogen metabolite ratios

benefits.specificClaim: Supports cervical dysplasia regression in CIN I-III

evidence.title: Double-blind randomized placebo-controlled multicenter clinical trial (phase IIa) on diindolylmethane's efficacy and safety in the treatment of CIN: implications for cervical cancer prevention
evidence.findings: Vaginal DIM suppositories at 100mg and 200mg/day showed significantly higher complete CIN regression rates vs placebo. Favorable safety profile with minimal adverse events.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled, multicenter Phase IIa trial for CIN I-II

evidence.title: Oral diindolylmethane (DIM): pilot evaluation of a nonsurgical treatment for cervical dysplasia
evidence.findings: 47% of DIM group showed improved dysplasia at first follow-up. 56% had improved colposcopic findings. Well-tolerated with no significant toxicity. However, no statistically significant difference between DIM and placebo groups.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind randomized pilot in women with CIN 2/3

evidence.title: Effect of diindolylmethane supplementation on low-grade cervical cytological abnormalities: double-blind, randomised, controlled trial
evidence.findings: CIN2+ rate: 9% DIM vs 12% placebo (RR 0.7). CIN3+ rate: 4.6% vs 5.1% (RR 0.9). Well-tolerated but unlikely to significantly affect cytology or HPV infection at 150mg/day. Largest DIM cervical trial to date.
evidence.methodology: Large double-blind RCT in women with low-grade cervical abnormalities

benefits.specificClaim: Chemopreventive properties through estrogen metabolism modulation and BRCA1 upregulation
benefits.replicationStatus: Biomarker evidence only; no cancer outcome trials

evidence.title: Chemopreventive properties of 3,3'-diindolylmethane in breast cancer: evidence from experimental and human studies
evidence.findings: Comprehensive evidence review supporting DIM chemopreventive properties through estrogen metabolism modulation. Epidemiological evidence supports cruciferous vegetable consumption and reduced breast cancer risk. However, no clinical trial directly demonstrates DIM efficacy for cancer treatment.
evidence.methodology: Review of experimental and human studies on DIM in breast cancer prevention

evidence.title: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial of diindolylmethane for breast cancer biomarker modulation in patients taking tamoxifen
evidence.findings: Favorable estrogen metabolism shift but no change in breast density (a cancer risk biomarker). Tamoxifen metabolites significantly reduced, raising concern about co-administration. BRCA1 modulation pathway supported.
evidence.methodology: 12-month double-blind RCT in breast cancer patients on tamoxifen

benefits.specificClaim: Anti-androgenic activity and PSA modulation in prostate cancer
benefits.replicationStatus: Phase I data only; limited tissue penetration observed

evidence.title: A phase I dose-escalation study of oral BR-DIM (BioResponse 3,3'-Diindolylmethane) in castrate-resistant, non-metastatic prostate cancer
evidence.findings: Minimal toxicity at doses up to 300mg. MTD 300mg, RP2D 225mg BID. 1/12 patients had 50% PSA decline; 1/12 had PSA stabilization. Modest clinical signal requiring further investigation.
evidence.methodology: Phase I dose-escalation in castrate-resistant prostate cancer

evidence.title: Phase Ib placebo-controlled, tissue biomarker trial of diindolylmethane (BR-DIMNG) in patients with prostate cancer who are undergoing prostatectomy
evidence.findings: DIM detected in only 7/28 prostate tissue specimens, suggesting poor tissue penetration. AR staining reduced in 37.5% of DIM-treated specimens. Urinary estrone ratio showed dose-dependent changes.
evidence.methodology: Randomized placebo-controlled Phase Ib in pre-prostatectomy patients

safety.finding: Well-tolerated at doses up to 300mg/day with minimal adverse effects

evidence.title: Single-dose pharmacokinetics and tolerability of absorption-enhanced 3,3'-diindolylmethane in healthy subjects
evidence.findings: No DIM-related adverse effects at doses up to 200mg. At 300mg, one subject reported vomiting probably related to study agent. Absorption-enhanced BR-DIM formulation achieved 50% higher plasma levels than crystalline DIM. Tmax approximately 2-3 hours.
evidence.methodology: Single-dose PK and tolerability study in healthy subjects

evidence.title: Effect of diindolylmethane supplementation on low-grade cervical cytological abnormalities: double-blind, randomised, controlled trial
evidence.findings: DIM 150mg/day well-tolerated over 6 months in the largest DIM clinical trial. No significant safety concerns reported in this population of women with low-grade cervical abnormalities.
evidence.methodology: 6-month large-scale double-blind RCT safety assessment

safety.finding: Significantly reduces tamoxifen and its active metabolites when co-administered

evidence.title: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial of diindolylmethane for breast cancer biomarker modulation in patients taking tamoxifen
evidence.findings: Tamoxifen, endoxifen, 4-OH tamoxifen, and N-desmethyl-tamoxifen levels were significantly reduced in DIM arm. This is a clinically important safety finding as endoxifen is the primary active metabolite of tamoxifen. Authors concluded further research needed before recommending DIM with tamoxifen.
evidence.methodology: 12-month RCT measuring tamoxifen metabolite pharmacokinetics

safety.finding: Isolated case reports of thrombotic events and visual disturbances at high doses

evidence.title: A phase I dose-escalation study of oral BR-DIM (BioResponse 3,3'-Diindolylmethane) in castrate-resistant, non-metastatic prostate cancer
evidence.findings: Two patients experienced grade 3 asymptomatic hyponatremia at higher doses. Overall minimal toxicity observed across dose cohorts up to 300mg. Systematic safety surveillance across 4 dose cohorts.
evidence.methodology: Phase I safety and tolerability assessment in prostate cancer patients
